name: Teknium
bio: Teknium (Ryan Teknium) is Co-founder and Head of Post-Training at Nous Research, an open-source AI lab focused on advancing foundational models through community-driven research. He is best known as the creator of the Hermes series of fine-tuned language models and the OpenHermes datasets, which have been collectively downloaded over 55 million times and power more than 120 applications. Before co-founding Nous Research in 2023, he worked at Stability AI. Starting from early GPT-4-generated instruction datasets like GPTeacher, he pioneered large-scale synthetic data curation for LLM post-training, scaling from 242K examples in OpenHermes 1 to over 5 million samples in Hermes 4. His work on neutrally-aligned, highly steerable instruct models has made the Hermes family one of the most widely adopted open-source fine-tune lines in the LLM ecosystem.

timeline.event: Created GitHub account and began releasing AI tools including alpaca-discord and AlpacaDataCleaned, entering the open-source LLM community
Released GPTeacher, a collection of modular GPT-4-generated datasets (General-Instruct, Roleplay, Code-Instruct, Toolformer) that became a foundational resource for LLM fine-tuning
Co-founded Nous Research with Jeffrey Quesnelle, Karan Malhotra, and Shivani Mitra as an open-source AI research collective
Released OpenHermes 7B and 13B, the first open-source Hermes fine-tunes trained on 242K entries of primarily GPT-4-generated data
Released OpenHermes 2 Mistral 7B with improved training data and ChatML prompt format support
Released OpenHermes 2.5 Mistral 7B, trained on 1M high-quality synthetic samples, achieving 50.7% on HumanEval and becoming the most downloaded OpenHermes model
Partnered with Together Compute to release StripedHyena-Nous-7B, combining Hermes data with the novel StripedHyena sub-quadratic architecture
Nous Research raised $5.2M seed round co-led by Distributed Global and OSS Capital, transitioning from volunteer project to funded company
Published Hermes 3 Technical Report (arXiv 2408.11857) and released Hermes 3 on Llama 3.1 (8B, 70B, 405B), achieving state-of-the-art among open-weight models
Released DeepHermes 3 Preview, one of the first models to unify deep reasoning (chain-of-thought) and traditional response modes in a single model
Nous Research raised $65M ($50M Series A led by Paradigm) at approximately $1B token valuation to expand R&D and build the Psyche decentralized training network
Published Hermes 4 Technical Report and released Hermes 4 models (70B, 405B), scaling post-training corpus from 1M to ~5M samples / ~60B tokens with stronger reasoning and schema-adherent outputs

key_contributions.title: Hermes Model Family
key_contributions.description: Flagship series of neutrally-aligned, highly steerable instruct-tuned language models released through Nous Research, spanning Hermes, Hermes 2, Hermes 3 (Llama 3.1), DeepHermes 3, and Hermes 4. Collectively downloaded over 55 million times and powering 120+ applications. Known for strong system prompt adherence and tool-use capabilities.

key_contributions.title: OpenHermes Datasets & Models
key_contributions.description: Open-source series of fine-tuned models and their training datasets, starting with 242K GPT-4-generated examples in OpenHermes 1 and scaling to 1M samples in OpenHermes 2.5. The OpenHermes 2.5 Mistral 7B model became one of the most popular open-source fine-tunes, with 152K+ downloads on Hugging Face.

key_contributions.title: GPTeacher
key_contributions.description: Early collection of modular GPT-4-generated datasets including General-Instruct, Roleplay, Code-Instruct, and Toolformer modules. Over 1,600 GitHub stars. Became a foundational dataset component in the OpenHermes training pipeline and influenced the broader synthetic data movement for LLM fine-tuning.

key_contributions.title: Nous Research
key_contributions.description: Co-founded open-source AI research lab in 2023 that grew from a volunteer collective into a $65M-funded company. Produces the Hermes model family, Psyche decentralized training network, and Atropos RL environments framework. Serves as Head of Post-Training, overseeing all fine-tuning, alignment, and RLHF pipelines.

key_contributions.title: Prompt Engineering Toolkit
key_contributions.description: Popular open-source toolkit for prompt engineering with over 420 GitHub stars, providing structured approaches to crafting effective prompts for large language models.

quotes.text: I saw Alpaca, and I wanted to remake it with GPT-4.
quotes.source: ThursdAI Podcast - Interview with Nous Research

quotes.text: Post-training takes pre-trained models and molds them into a smarter, more steerable, and fine-tuned version of it, which can be aligned to the morals or ethics you want, or built specifically for working with your product or other tooling.
quotes.source: Delphi Intelligence AMA with Nous Research
